Title: Tom Lake
Author: Ann Patchett
My Review:
- had I known this was going to be partially set during covid, I would not have read. It was too heavy handed and didn’t really add anything to the story other than …. Family time?? Felt like a very shallow interpretation of what we were going through at that time and I really don’t see what it added to the story of Lara and duke
- none of the characters were interesting …. Duke was irritating and I didn’t get a sense of the depth of his and Lara’s relationship. I don’t feel like I understood any of the characters’ personalities/motives, everyone seemed to have the same thoughts
- lots of telling and not showing. come on!!
- the orchard stuff could have been a cool way to add something interesting to the book but seemed like a relatively static background to the story. They picked cherries into lugs onto the gator a thousand times, which I understand is the monotony of the work but at least say something more interesting about it

Title: Icebreaker (UCMH, #1)
Author: Hannah  Grace
My Review:
Tolerable (until you realize it’s 400 pages for no reason) if you overlook:
- far too many men in this book. Sabrina was supposed to be her bestie and yet they shared like 2 conversations and they were always about the boys ….I’m not even sure if this book passes the bechdel test
- Aaron being overtly evil for no damn reason and anastasia continuing to want to live with him???!!!
- also I think she would have actually died if she was eating so little while skating/lifting 6 days a week so that doesn’t add up
- the paragraphs (and paragraphs) of telling not showing …. Come on girl remember high school English class
- conveniently, all of the non white/non straight characters were side characters!
- that godawful epilogue. Why is she pregnant at 23. Why do they have a dog. Why are they getting married. Why do all romance novels have to end with marriage and/or fertility
- I don’t understand why this author chose to write about college kids in California (with the most Michigan ass midwestern name for a fictional UC) because the English slang/phrases in the characters’ speech and povs were jarring
- Nathan driving a tesla

A few redeeming qualities
- importance of consent!
- anastasia finally screaming at Aaron and leaving him behind ……… 350 pages too late
- the hockey team interactions w each other and the girls were cute
